title: Timeline
subtitle: Milestones in Io's history, from 2002 to today.

items.title: Io created
items.subtitle: Steve Dekorte begins work on Io as a minimal prototype-based language.

items.title: Mailing list
items.subtitle: The iolanguage Yahoo! Group becomes the gathering place for early users and patch exchanges.

items.title: First public release
items.subtitle: The earliest public Io releases and programming guide appear, distributed through the mailing list.

items.title: Actors and coroutines
items.subtitle: Beta 2002-08-11 adds concurrency: Actors (object-oriented threads) implemented with coroutines and cooperative yield.

items.title: Addon system
items.subtitle: First addon libraries land: Directory, XML (expat), MD5, Blowfish, regex, and OpenGL/GLUT — establishing the addon model that grows rapidly over the next year.

items.title: Networking
items.subtitle: Sockets addon adds TCP servers and clients; UDP follows a week later. Async DNS arrives in November, and IoServer becomes a dedicated build target.

items.title: Incremental GC
items.subtitle: Mark-and-sweep is replaced with an incremental tri-color collector with write barrier — trading a small throughput hit for far better real-time pause behavior.

items.title: Weak links
items.subtitle: A WeakLink primitive is added, letting objects reference each other without preventing collection — useful for caches and back-pointers.

items.title: Addon explosion
items.subtitle: Rapid expansion: curses, JPEG/TIFF, zlib, Perl5 regex, Objective-C bridge, SGML, SQLite, and more — Io becomes usable for real-world scripting and embedding.

items.title: Futures
items.subtitle: @ returns a transparent future that becomes its result when touched; @@ fires and forgets. Deadlocks are detected by walking the future graph.

items.title: Vector primitive (SIMD)
items.subtitle: A numeric Vector type with AltiVec acceleration lands. Strings, buffers, and numeric vectors unify under Sequence.

items.title: Lua Workshop talk
items.subtitle: Steve presents Io at the Lua Workshop, contrasting message-tree semantics with Lua’s table-based model.

items.title: OOPSLA / DLS
items.subtitle: Io is presented at the ACM Dynamic Languages Symposium, co-located with OOPSLA’05 in San Diego, bringing the language to a wider research audience.

items.title: Public DARCS repository
items.subtitle: A public source repository hosted at iolanguage.com/darcs/Io replaces the email-patch workflow.

items.title: Migration to git
items.subtitle: Source history moves from DARCS to git, hosted on iolanguage.com.

items.title: Published on GitHub
items.subtitle: The Io repository appears at github.com/IoLanguage/io, making issues and pull requests the primary contribution channel.

items.title: Portable ucontext
items.subtitle: Russ Cox’s portable ucontext replaces platform-specific coroutine code, enabling cross-platform support including 64-bit macOS.

items.title: CMake build system
items.subtitle: The build moves from hand-written Makefiles to CMake, simplifying ports and addon builds.

items.title: Io Book on Apple iBooks
items.subtitle: Steve Dekorte publishes Io on the Apple iBooks Store on 2012-01-23 — a compact introduction to the language, prototypes, messages, and concurrency.

items.title: Eerie package manager
items.subtitle: Josip Lisec’s Eerie package manager is rewritten by Ales Tsurko under the IoLanguage org, with heavy activity through 2021.

items.title: ARM64 coroutines
items.subtitle: Native ARM64 coroutine support lands for Apple Silicon and modern Linux.

items.title: Stackless evaluator
items.subtitle: The recursive C-stack evaluator is replaced with a heap-allocated frame state machine — enabling TCO, robust exception unwinding, and first-class continuations.

items.title: Resumable exceptions
items.subtitle: Common Lisp–style signal / withHandler: handlers can resume, fall through, or unwind — made practical by the stackless evaluator.

items.title: WASM/WASI port
items.subtitle: Io cross-compiles to WebAssembly. A bidirectional Io↔JavaScript bridge replaces the native addon model.

items.title: Async/await bridge
items.subtitle: Io futures tie into JavaScript Promises: asyncSend and future touches yield the eval loop across the JS boundary, and unrecognized messages on a future implicitly await the result.

items.title: BigInt support
items.subtitle: A BigInt primitive arrives with arbitrary-precision arithmetic and transparent round-tripping across the Io↔JS bridge, aligning Io numerics with modern JavaScript.

items.title: SIMD128 Vector backend
items.subtitle: The Vector primitive gains a WebAssembly SIMD128 backend, restoring the AltiVec-era vector acceleration in the browser and on modern hardware via WASM.

items.title: WASM merged to master
items.subtitle: The WASM branch becomes the default target. Book and site restructured for the new era.
